user_query:
I’m preparing a weekly transparency post for our community about the health of our CI/CD pipeline for the advocacy site. Using the input files ci/pipeline.yaml, config/app.yaml, and logs/pipeline_runs.csv in this workspace, please do the following and produce two outputs under out/:

1) Generate a machine-readable CI summary at out/ci_summary.json with these fields:
- deploy_env: string extracted from ci/pipeline.yaml (env.DEPLOY_ENV)
- site_name: string extracted from config/app.yaml (site.name)
- time_window: object with start, end (ISO8601 strings from the earliest and latest timestamps in logs/pipeline_runs.csv) and total_runs (integer count of all runs for configured jobs only)
- jobs: an object keyed by job name (from ci/pipeline.yaml jobs[].name). Each value must include runs, successes, failures, success_rate (0–1 float), and avg_duration_seconds (float) computed from logs/pipeline_runs.csv filtered to that job
- jobs_in_logs_not_in_config: array of job names present in logs but missing from the CI config
- jobs_in_config_without_runs: array of configured job names that have zero runs in the logs
- overall: object with runs, success_rate (0–1 float), and avg_duration_seconds (float) computed across configured jobs only

2) Write a plain-language status update at out/weekly_status.md for our followers (community-focused, not just engineers) that includes:
- A title line that mentions the site_name and deploy_env
- A date range line with the same start and end from the summary
- A brief paragraph (at least two sentences) summarizing the CI/CD health
- A job-by-job bullet list showing each configured job’s success_rate (as a percentage) and avg_duration_seconds
- A Notable issues section listing: (a) any configured job with a failure rate above 20% (include its failure count and runs), (b) any jobs_in_logs_not_in_config, and (c) any jobs_in_config_without_runs. If none apply, include the sentence: "No notable issues detected this period."

Rules and constraints:
- Treat the list of configured jobs and DEPLOY_ENV as authoritative from ci/pipeline.yaml. Use config/app.yaml only for the site name. Do not hardcode job names.
- Compute statistics solely from logs/pipeline_runs.csv. Include only configured jobs in the per-job and overall metrics. Use exact timestamps from the CSV to define the time window.
- Do not call external services. All work must be done locally with the provided files.
- Ensure both output files are created at the exact paths specified: out/ci_summary.json and out/weekly_status.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
